instruction: Could you start VS Code in folder ~/Desktop/project from the terminal?
config: _execute_setup(command=['/bin/bash', '-c', 'cd /home/user/Downloads && unzip -q vscodeEvalExtension.zip && code --install-extension vscodeEvalExtension/eval-0.0.1.vsix && rm -rf vscodeEvalExtension vscodeEvalExtension.zip && mkdir -p ~/Desktop/project/.vscode && history -c && echo > ~/.bash_history'])

evaluator.eval: check_include_exclude(get_vm_command_line(env, config={'command': ['/bin/bash', '-c', 'use_terminal=$(cat ~/.bash_history | grep "[c]ode "); if [ -z "$use_terminal" ]; then echo "false"; else echo "true"; fi']}), get_rule(env, config={'rules': {'include': ['true']}})) and compare_config(get_vscode_config(env, config={'vscode_extension_command': 'OpenProject', 'path': '/home/user/OpenProject.txt', 'dest': 'OpenProject.txt'}), get_rule(env, config={'rules': {'expected': 'project'}}))
